message:
feat(frankenpandas): expand prelude with merge family (br-dghe4 / fd90.143)

fd90.143: README Recipes 'Merge-Asof for Time Series Alignment' (lines
1338-1346) imports prelude::* and calls merge_asof + AsofDirection,
neither of which were in the prelude. A copy-paste hits:
  error[E0425]: cannot find function 'merge_asof' in this scope
  error[E0433]: failed to resolve: use of undeclared type 'AsofDirection'

Continues the prelude-completeness pattern from fd90.121/125/126/127/
128/130/141/142.

Fix: add the merge family to the prelude:
  Types:    AsofDirection, JoinError, MergedDataFrame
  Functions: join_series, merge_asof, merge_dataframes_on

All already pub-used at the top level (crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs:
167-168). MergedDataFrame is the return type of both merge_asof and
merge_with_options (per the fd90.113 + fd90.137 README notes), so
having it in the prelude lets users type-annotate intermediate bindings.

Verified:
- cargo build -p frankenpandas — compiles
- cargo test -p frankenpandas --lib — all 6 tests pass

Pure re-export expansion. No behavior change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-devw","title":"Add dataframe diff/pct_change/rank/quantile null hardened coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:48:16.777190155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:48:57.614855418Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:48:57.612361607Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-df9v7","title":"fd90.138: add where_mask_df_other to mirror mask_df_other (closes API asymmetry noted in fd90.136)","description":"fd90.74-137 README audit. fd90.136 documented an API asymmetry — there's mask_df_other (DataFrame-other variant for mask) at crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:18591, but no where_mask_df_other. Both where_mask_df and mask_df only accept &Scalar.

This is a code-level gap. pandas' where()/mask() both support either a scalar or a DataFrame as the 'other' argument. FrankenPandas covers the scalar case for both and the DataFrame case for mask only — leaving where_mask_df_other as a missing convenience.

Fix: add 'pub fn where_mask_df_other(&self, cond: &Self, other: &Self) -> Result<Self, FrameError>' to fp-frame, mirroring mask_df_other (line 18591). The semantics:
  - where cond is True  → keep self's value
  - where cond is False → use other's value (aligned)
  - missing cond → NaN

Verify with cargo build -p fp-frame + cargo test -p fp-frame --lib (subset, since the workspace is large). Add a unit test that exercises the new function with a DataFrame replacement source.

Pure additive API expansion. No behavior change to existing functions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T12:56:55.102487077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T12:59:31.282681900Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T12:59:31.279817586Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-dgfr","title":"pandas: DataFrame.apply('product', axis=0|1) alias parity","description":"DataFrame::apply_named() recognizes 'prod' but not the pandas alias 'product'. Add alias support for both axis=0 and axis=1 in crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs, with regressions proving it matches the existing product semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:24:37.221930595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:41:55.259072789Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:41:55.257182299Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-dghe4","title":"fd90.143: merge_asof + AsofDirection used in README but not in prelude (continues fd90.142 pattern)","description":"fd90.74-142 README audit. Recipes 'Merge-Asof for Time Series Alignment' example at lines 1338-1346 imports 'use frankenpandas::prelude::*' (line 1305 of the broader Recipes section) and calls:
  let merged = merge_asof(&trades, &quotes, "timestamp", AsofDirection::Backward)?;

Both merge_asof and AsofDirection are pub-used at the top level (crates/frankenpandas/src/lib.rs:167-168) but NOT in the prelude. Verified with grep — neither name appears in the 'pub mod prelude' block. Plus merge_dataframes_on, join_series, MergedDataFrame are similarly top-level only.

A user copy-pasting the merge_asof example from prelude::* hits:
  error[E0425]: cannot find function 'merge_asof' in this scope
  error[E0433]: failed to resolve: use of undeclared type 'AsofDirection'

Continues the prelude-completeness pattern (fd90.121/125/126/127/128/130/141/142). Fix: add the merge family to the prelude:
  AsofDirection
  JoinError (for users matching join errors)
  MergedDataFrame (return type of merge_asof / merge_with_options)
  join_series
  merge_asof
  merge_dataframes_on

Verify with cargo build + cargo test -p frankenpandas --lib.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:37:00.268459303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:38:50.368828519Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:38:50.366102194Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-diic","title":"[REALITY-CHECK HIGH] No fp-python crate; drop-in pandas claim unbacked","description":"ls crates shows 12 crates with no fp-python or fp-pyo3. README FAQ admits PyO3 bindings planned future step. Drop-in replacement claim unbacked. Disposition needed: ship fp-python or strip drop-in positioning until bindings ship.","notes":"cc-pandas triage 2026-04-22 (commit 2736f13): qualified the two Python-implying claims in README.md. Hero tagline now reads 'Drop-in pandas API in safe Rust — today. Python bindings (import frankenpandas) planned via PyO3; see the Roadmap.' and the Polars FAQ answer now explicitly scopes 'Python drop-in requires the planned PyO3 bindings; today FrankenPandas is a Rust library.' The defensible claim (pandas-API-parity-in-Rust, verified by the conformance oracle) stays; the Python-level drop-in implication is removed until the fp-python / PyO3 crate ships. Strip-not-ship path taken per the bead's own disposition choices.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:26.569427150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:04.326593159Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:04.323809962Z","closed_by_session":"cc-pandas-diic","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
